Hiring teams

YouTube video editor job description

YouTube editor roles need more than software requirements. The post should explain channel format, pacing, cadence, references, analytics expectations, and review workflow.

Define the channel workflow

State whether the editor is cutting talking-head videos, podcasts, faceless explainers, documentaries, tutorials, shorts, or repurposed livestreams. Each format has different proof requirements.

Name the deliverables

Include target runtime, weekly or monthly output, shorts or clips, captions, graphics, thumbnails, sponsor reads, music, sound cleanup, color, and export requirements.

Ask for relevant portfolio clips

Request two or three YouTube examples with notes about the editor's role. If retention, hooks, or pacing matter, say that directly so editors can send the right samples.

Need an editor?

Send a hiring brief and start the early matching queue.
